summary: Completed full end-to-end frontend testing of the 5-act 'RACKSHEETTH: THE MEMORY VAULT' cinematic experience. All 28 assertions across Act I (Hero+Capsule), Act II (Vault password gate), Act III (Countdown+Confetti), Act IV (Inbox cascade + Message viewer + read engine), and Act V (Finale + return-to-hero with completed state) passed successfully. Vault tested with wrong password (denied) and case-insensitive correct password ('geburtstag'). All 16 notifications cascaded, were opened/closed, marked read, unread count decremented correctly, and finale auto-fired ~700ms after the last close.

issues: Minor cosmetic: residual ghost glyphs of previous title words remain faintly visible behind the staggered title reveal (visible in screenshot as 'any', 'rday', 'tth!!!' under main words). May be intentional cinematic layering, but if not, the previous-line opacity should fall to 0 fully.

action_items: Optional polish: verify the staggered hero title animation fully fades out prior-line ghost text in Hero.jsx if not intentional.
No functional fixes required — all spec-required flows are working end-to-end.
critical_code_review_comments: App.js state machine is clean and minimal; auto-finale trigger correctly gated on act===INBOX, readIds.size===total, !completed.
Hero is unmounted/remounted via showHero (not always mounted as the inline comment suggests) — comment is slightly misleading but behavior is correct.
Password compare is case-insensitive via toUpperCase as specified; PASSWORD constant centralized in /app/frontend/src/data/wishes.js.

All required data-testid attributes are present and correctly wired (hero-screen, hero-title, unread-capsule, unread-capsule-label, vault-screen, vault-password-input, vault-unlock-button, vault-error, countdown-screen, countdown-num-3/2/1, countdown-celebrate, confetti-canvas, inbox-screen, inbox-unread-count, notification-1..16 with data-read, message-viewer, viewer-close, typewriter-text, finale-screen, finale-19, finale-memories, finale-typewriter).

Confetti canvas triggers a Chrome console warning: 'Canvas2D: Multiple readback operations using getImageData are faster with the willReadFrequently attribute set to true.' Non-blocking but trivially fixable by passing { willReadFrequently: true } to getContext('2d').
test_credentials: Vault password: GEBURTSTAG (verified case-insensitive — 'geburtstag' also unlocks).
seed_data_creation: None — frontend resets every page load, no persistence.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Full 5-act flow verified working end-to-end including: hero loads with RACK.png + animated title + '16 Unread Messages' capsule; vault rejects wrong password with shake/error and accepts case-insensitive correct one; countdown 3-2-1 + confetti + 'Nineteen' celebration plays; inbox cascades all 16 notifications in ~9s; message viewer opens with shared-layout transform, shows [WISHER_NAME_N] + [PHOTO_PLACEHOLDER] + typewriter body; closing marks data-read='true' and decrements inbox-unread-count; reading all 16 auto-triggers finale within ~700ms; finale plays 19 → memories → typewriter then returns to hero with capsule label 'You Have Read All Messages'. Only minor cosmetic note about ghost title glyphs in hero — not a functional bug.
rca of the issue: No issues to RCA — all spec-required flows pass.
